text:
Hello! I'm Braden. Trained as a biomedical engineer and spent my career developing medical devices. I'm currently an innovation fellow at the Medical Device Center at the University of Minnesota. It's a year-long leadership program to find an unmet medical problem and come up with a new device to address it. I'm was referred to the FoC slack from the Julia Discourse forum.

My main interest is accelerating the rate at which we (everyone) develop products. The frustrating reality for many product designers is solving a problem that has been solved before ... but the solution is inaccessible (paywalls, trade secrets, lost, forgotten, poorly documented). Some people shrug and say "that's just the way it works." I see this as an avoidable waste of the time and energy of *many* engineers, scientists, and designers. I think programming is a key element to addressing this problem.

<@U01TG6EBTNX>, I enjoyed your talk at JuliaCon 2020. I met a few people from relationalAI for beers ages ago, and it seems like you've curated an incredibly talented team.

text:
<@UA14TGLTC> Fake books and the Real Book are an interesting case of super schematic notation, lacking almost everything that's involved with performance, but nonetheless useful (enough context to sit in and "play the head" or to serve as a starting point for writing out more complete arrangements).

In comparison, the charts I'd write for my old NYC early jazz project involved much more structure and formality, even though there was loads of improvisation going on:
<https://vimeo.com/17852041>

attachments.title: Clarinet Marmalade
attachments.text:
Epistrophy Swing Orchestra

Jason Candler: alto clarinet
Luca Fadda: trumpet
Joe Keady: tuba
Quince Marcum: drums/vocals
Jack Rusher: guitar

... and special guest J. Walter Hawkes on trombone.
attachments.video_html: <iframe src="https://player.vimeo.com/video/17852041?autoplay=1&app_id=122963" width="320" height="240" frameborder="0" allow="autoplay; fullscreen; picture-in-picture" allowfullscreen title="Clarinet Marmalade"></iframe>

text: Hi Molham, I watched your talk and found it fascinating. Are the slides available anywhere? I'm interested in followup up on the references you provide in the last few slides. I really like how you can implement various knowledge representations approaches with a simple common underlying model. Actually, I just found: <https://www.relational.ai/research> which probably has links to what I'm looking for.

text:
How does retionalAI's apporach to knowledge graphs compare to Grakn?

Also, are there ways in relationAI to make public knowledge graphs built for interoperability? It seems to me that rAI is mostly for use within a single institution.

I spoke with Barry Smith a while back about his work developing an ISO standard for top-level ontologies—ISO/IEC 21838. He has some great points about what makes a shared ontology grow and survive (eg, the gene ontology).

elements.text:
How does retionalAI's apporach to knowledge graphs compare to Grakn?

Also, are there ways in relationAI to make public knowledge graphs built for interoperability? It seems to me that rAI is mostly for use within a single institution.

I spoke with Barry Smith a while back about his work developing an ISO standard for top-level ontologies—ISO/IEC 21838. He has some great points about what makes a shared ontology grow and survive (eg, the gene ontology).

text: I'll second <@U013ZLJARC7>'s "dirt off your shoulder" sentiments. But I'll add that I don't especially begrudge people who "dump on Dynamicland without going there", since 99.9% of people _can't_ go there, and Dynamicland certainly hasn't prioritized thorough public documentation. (I think some criticism of that is valid!) Thankfully, the goal of the research isn't to have people say nice things about it in Hacker News. :grinning:

text:
<@U01T8PXSJJW> I feel like we’re separated at birth from Grakn :slightly_smiling_face:.  We seen to be philosophically very similar but have taken different approaches with our architecture and systems engineering.  It would be great to connect with someone from Grakn to compare notes as our interests are very aligned with theirs: we both believe in the value of Knowledge Graphs.
I’ve watched a few of Barry Smith’s talks but I don’t remember anything about an ISO standard.  Do you have any links you can sent me?

text:
Hey friends, it's been sometime since I posted an update on Gadget. For those unfamiliar with Gadget, we're trying to build a development platform that steps in and handles the bs boilerplate/repetitive stuff for you, but then steps out of your way and lets you code when you prefer. The platform is nearing completion and launch, if anyone is interested in playing with the alpha, ping me.

This demo is. a bit longer (20 min) but in that time, I build and productionize a full set of APIs to power a kanban board (with auth, access control and the works) and run some graphQL mutations to show that its all real.

<https://www.loom.com/share/732816bb327c4155b61199d2d1733c56>

Feedback welcome!

attachments.title: Create a new App - Gadget
attachments.fallback: Loom Video: Create a new App - Gadget

For those unfamiliar with Gadget, we're trying to build a development platform that steps in and handles the bs boilerplate/repetitive stuff for you, but then steps out of your way and lets you code when you prefer. The platform is nearing completion and launch, if anyone is interested in playing with the alpha, ping me.

This demo is. a bit longer (20 min) but in that time, I build and productionize a full set of APIs to power a kanban board (with auth, access control and the works) and run some graphQL mutations to show that its all real.

Here's a link to one of Barry's talks on the ISO 21838: <https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=aux_zcK7XSI>. The standard is still under development but should be out soon.
Grakn Orbit is coming up in 11 days at that'a a good chance for you to connect with the Grakn team. I don't use Grakn, but I'm keeping a close eye on it's development. I'll probably watch a selection of the talks at Orbit.  <https://community.grakn.ai/grakn-orbit-2021>.

text:
[moved from top level, original post by <@U01DX39SB33>]

For the statechart enthusiasts, go to ~6 min and watch our take on Harel's statechart which we modified to accomodate CRUD itself
So we're using the statechart to manage all of your application's logic, including CRUD.
